intro: Stephanodaphne capitata is a tropical plant species native to West Africa, belonging to the family Lauraceae and commonly known as African stephanodaphne. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its numerous health benefits, including treating fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Stephanodaphne capitata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional African medicine, Stephanodaphne capitata is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and is also used in the production of essential oils and extracts for various herbal remedies.
